artifacts.content:
Hi Mum,

Just grabbing ten quiet minutes with a cuppa before evening prep and wanted to put a few things in writing so you don’t worry.

Money first: the standing order for the £450 is still going out on the 1st of every month, same as it has since June 2023. We’re finally at the point where the bistro feels properly back in the black after that horrible spike in bills in March 2023, when you and Dad remortgaged a bit of the Carlton bungalow and lent me the £18,000 to cover three months’ rent and the worst of the supplier invoices. I still feel sick thinking how close we were to losing The Green Hearth, but also weirdly proud that we’ve pulled it round without missing a single repayment to you.

Do you remember the ‘Founders’ Night’ we did last October with Dad’s slow‑roast lamb and your lemon drizzle on the menu? I’ve had three separate regulars ask when we’re doing another one, so I’m thinking of making it an annual thing and using it as a marker for how far we’ve come each year.

On the less cheerful side, Dad’s next check‑up with the oncology team at Bassetlaw Hospital is coming up, isn’t it? If it lands on a Friday, I can move the Quiet Table lunch we’ve been doing once a month since 11 November 2022 so I can come and sit with you both. Those chemo days are grim enough without hospital canteen sandwiches.

Anyway, I’d better get back to the veg prep. Tell Dad the courgettes he dropped off have gone into tomorrow’s soup.

Love,
Em x

metadata.subject: Money, Dad, and a quick catch-up

artifacts.content:
Hey Rach,

You’re on late again with the community team tonight so I’m typing this at the pass while the stock simmers.

I’ve just blocked out the next run of CBT sessions with Karen Douglas at Bassetlaw Hospital – first Tuesday of the month, 3–4pm, same as we’ve done since January 2023. It still feels strange thinking back to that Friday dinner service in November 2022 when I had the full‑on panic attack on the pass at The Green Hearth and Tom had to take over while I stood outside on Newcastle Avenue trying to breathe. Karen keeps circling back to that night, and to the weeks we did the four‑day service model from 6 March to 28 May 2023 so I could actually be home with you in the evenings after Orchard Medical Practice signed you off with the severe anxiety and depression.

I know we were both terrified of what cutting to Wednesday–Saturday would do to the numbers, but it’s funny how much stuck: Mondays now are Community Table, quiet prep, and spreadsheets instead of late service, and I’m sure that’s part of why I haven’t completely burned out.

On the subject of looking after people: J. pulled me aside again yesterday. He’s still on the Bassetlaw Community Mental Health Team waiting list and says the self‑harming’s “not as bad” but I’ve kept his shifts finishing by 9:30pm like we agreed from 13 May. I’m covering two of his weekend closes myself this month and trialling those little ten‑minute check‑ins with all the juniors.

Can we have a no‑work, Milo‑heavy walk round Clumber on Sunday if you’re not on call? I think we both need it.

Love you,
E x

metadata.subject: Therapy Tuesdays & looking after the kids (and us)

artifacts.content:
Staff / money / headspace – running list

1. HMRC
- Time‑to‑pay from March 2021: £7,400 PAYE + VAT arrears spread over 12 months from 1 April 2021. Never again. Use it as the line in the sand.
- Waste‑weighing system that came out of it worked: 42kg/week down to 18kg/week by early 2022, and now we’re hovering around 11kg/week even with more covers.

2. Parents’ loan
- Mum & Dad remortgaged Carlton in Lindrick bungalow in March 2023 to lend £18k (3 months’ rent + suppliers) when energy bills went mad.
- Standing order £450/month since June 2023 – keep that sacred, like payroll and farmers.
- Next “Founders’ Night” – pencil in October; menu around their favourites + story of keeping the doors open.

3. Quiet Table / Community bits
- Quiet Table Friday lunches: started 11 Nov 2022 after Dad’s early‑stage bowel cancer diagnosis at Bassetlaw. One Friday lunch/month, pay‑what‑you‑can, we discreetly comp ~£60–£80 from profits.
- Cook & Chat at Gateford Road: remember May–Nov 2021, sending J. home with that extra £15–£20 bag of ingredients and the Monday veg box drop‑offs near Sandy Lane. Build that sort of support into the new youth partnership without burning myself out.

4. Safeguarding / juniors
- Kitchen porter J. – self‑harm disclosure May 2024, on Bassetlaw CMHT waiting list. Rota from 13/05: no finishes after 21:30, I cover at least two weekend shifts, introduce regular check‑ins for all under‑21s.

5. Non‑negotiables
- Real Living Wage, pay farmers on time, protect my therapy slot, at least one work‑free evening/week with Rach + Milo.

metadata.title: Big picture priorities – don’t lose sight

artifacts.content:
Hi Mark,

Hope you’re well and not drowning in year‑end paperwork.

Just a quick note to say that if there are any more evening delivery pilots like we did back in 2020, I’ll need to be a bit more organised about transport this time. You might remember in August 2020 I was stopped on Kilton Hill at about 11:45pm in my old silver Fiesta with an expired MOT while dropping off the last batch of hot meals to shielding residents. I got the formal warning and the £100 fixed penalty, paid it on 3 September 2020 and had the car off the road for three days for the MOT, which meant emailing you in a panic the next morning about combining two routes.

Lesson learned – the current car’s MOT is in the calendar with three reminders, and if we do anything similar again I’ll rota in a second driver so we’re not relying on my knackered motor and caffeine.

On a related note, the Community Table we’ve been running at The Green Hearth on Wednesdays has its roots in that whole period. We launched the Cook & Chat sessions with Lisa Turner’s youth group on Gateford Road in May 2021 off the back of the pandemic work, and I’ve kept up a quiet habit from then – from June to November 2021 I was dropping a weekly veg box to one lad’s council flat near Sandy Lane in my own car after Monday prep, plus sending him home with an extra £15–£20 of ingredients each session.

If the council ever wants to formalise a referral route into Community Table or Cook & Chat, happy to chat.

Best,
Emma

Emma Louise Cartwright
Owner & Head Chef, The Green Hearth Bistro

metadata.subject: Future meal deliveries & a thought about referrals

artifacts.content:
Event: CBT with Karen – service debrief & boundaries
Date: 2026-02-02 (and recurring first Tuesday monthly)
Time: 15:00–16:00
Location: Bassetlaw Hospital, Outpatient Psychology (with Karen Douglas)
Attendees: Emma Cartwright; Karen Douglas

Notes:
- Check‑in on anxiety around Friday and Saturday dinner services at The Green Hearth – especially memories of the November 2022 panic attack on the pass when Tom had to take over.
- Talk about how the March–May 2023 four‑day service pattern (Wed–Sat only, from 06/03/23 to 28/05/23) helped while Rach was signed off by Orchard Medical Practice in Mansfield with severe anxiety and depression. Explore what elements of that we can keep without tanking the business.
- Bring up emotional hangover from Grandma Mags’ death in Feb 2022 at Bassetlaw (respiratory ward) and using her funeral wake at Carlton in Lindrick Village Hall on 18/02/22 as the test run for the low‑waste, plant‑forward buffet – 120 portions cooked overnight at the bistro. Still not sure I’ve fully grieved vs just “catered it.”
- Discuss impact of Dad’s early‑stage bowel cancer diagnosis (Oct 2022, Bassetlaw oncology) and the monthly Quiet Table lunches we started on 11/11/22 for people post‑chemo – boundaries around how much of guests’ stories I carry home.
- Ask for strategies for supporting staff like J. (kitchen porter, self‑harm disclosure May 2024, on Bassetlaw CMHT waiting list) without turning into everyone’s unofficial therapist.

metadata.title: CBT with Karen – service, grief, boundaries

artifacts.content:
Hi Jon,

I know we mostly text these days about boring stuff like whether you can look at the dodgy light in the ladies’ loo, but I wanted to write properly.

I’ve been thinking a lot about that awful row we had at Mum and Dad’s in Carlton in Lindrick back in August 2020 over Gran Mags’ will and the smallholding. I hated how it felt like we were arguing about “wasting” her legacy, and I don’t think I ever told you how much that pushed me into getting the North Notts Community Kitchen Network off the ground.

On 10 January 2021 I sat at the laptop in the bistro office and emailed four local cafés/pubs about pooling surplus food – that’s what turned into the network we’ve got now. It was very much me trying to turn all that anger and sadness into something that would have made her proud.

Closing The Green Hearth for three days around her funeral in February 2022 so I could cook the 120‑portion low‑waste, mostly plant‑based buffet for the wake at Carlton in Lindrick Village Hall was part of the same thing. I spent the night of the 17th in the kitchen with her old enamel pans, reworking her smallholding recipes so nothing went in the bin.

Anyway, I don’t want us to keep circling that argument forever. Maybe next time you’re over in Worksop fixing my ancient extraction fan we can grab a coffee and talk about doing something small but concrete in her name – a little “Mags’ Plot” herb bed out back at the bistro, or a bursary for one of the Cook & Chat kids.

Love,
Em

metadata.subject: Gran, the row, and what I did with it

artifacts.content:
Service / community planning – brain dump

1. Cook & Chat (Gateford Road youth centre)
- Started May 2021 with Lisa Turner’s charity. Remember the 17‑year‑old who told us he’d been skipping meals because his mum’s Universal Credit was sanctioned.
- From June–Nov 2021: quietly sent him home every session with extra ingredients worth ~£15–£20 and did a weekly veg box drop at their council flat near Sandy Lane after Monday prep, using my own car.
- For 2026, formalise a small hardship pot so it’s not just me absorbing it.

2. Financial resilience
- HMRC time‑to‑pay (March 2021) and the £7,400 PAYE/VAT arrears forced the waste‑tracking and menu engineering that actually made us stronger.
- Parents’ £18k loan (March 2023, remortgaged Carlton bungalow) + £450/month repayments from June 2023 – non‑negotiable.

3. Staff welfare systems
- Kitchen porter J. (self‑harm disclosure May 2024, on Bassetlaw Community Mental Health Team waiting list) – rota tweaks from 13/05 so no shifts past 21:30, I personally covered two weekend shifts. Expand check‑in system for all juniors (traffic‑light mood board? anonymous suggestion box?).

4. Quiet Table / cancer links
- Dad’s early‑stage bowel cancer (Oct 2022, Bassetlaw) – monthly “Quiet Table” Friday lunch from 11/11/22, pay‑what‑you‑can, comp £60–£80 per sitting.

5. Personal boundaries
- Protect CBT with Karen Douglas (Bassetlaw Hospital, monthly since Jan 2023) – don’t book suppliers’ meetings over it.
- At least one evening/week fully off with Rach and Milo – no rota tweaks, no emails, no spreadsheets.
